title: aquilegia bauhini subsp. bertolonii
intro: Aquilegia bauhini subsp. bertolonii, also known as Granny's Bonnet or Bergenia, is a perennial plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Aquilegia bauhini subsp. bertolonii include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
